_comment: ArmaField MapSeeding-aware Arma Reforger egg. Based on pelican-eggs/games-steamcmd Arma Reforger egg by red_thirten. Adds: MapSeeding-driven scenarioId rotation, ArmaField mod baked into default config, BackendSettings.json stub.
name: Arma Reforger - ArmaField
author: contact@armafield.gg
description:
Arma Reforger Dedicated Server with the ArmaField mod and automatic mission rotation via the mod's MapSeeding.json. After each mission the server exits, Wings restarts the container, and our hook reads MapSeeding.json (written by the mod) to pick the next scenarioId before launch.

Backend: defaults point at the public test backend (https://test.armafield.gg). Use the Backend Token and Backend URL panel variables to point at your own self-hosted backend (https://github.com/ArmaField/ArmaField-BackEnd) for production play. URL must be https:// - Arma Reforger rejects plain http.

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash

## File: ArmaField Pelican Egg installer
## Based on: pelican-eggs/games-steamcmd/arma/arma_reforger by red_thirten
## License: MIT
## Image: ghcr.io/parkervcp/installers:debian

set -e
export HOME=/mnt/server
cd /tmp
mkdir -p $HOME/steamcmd $HOME/steamapps
curl -sSL -o steamcmd.tar.gz https://steamcdn-a.akamaihd.net/client/installer/steamcmd_linux.tar.gz
tar -xzvf steamcmd.tar.gz -C $HOME/steamcmd
cd $HOME/steamcmd

# Workaround for experimental branch
if [[ "${SRCDS_APPID}" == "1890870" ]]; then
    WINDOWS_INSTALL="1"
fi

chown -R root:root /mnt

# Install/verify game server
./steamcmd.sh +force_install_dir $HOME +login anonymous $( [[ "${WINDOWS_INSTALL}" == "1" ]] && printf %s '+@sSteamCmdForcePlatformType windows' ) +app_update ${SRCDS_APPID} +app_update 1007 $( [[ -z ${SRCDS_BETAID} ]] || printf %s "-beta ${SRCDS_BETAID}" ) $( [[ -z ${SRCDS_BETAPASS} ]] || printf %s "-betapassword ${SRCDS_BETAPASS}" ) validate +quit

mkdir -p /mnt/server/.steam/sdk{32,64}
cp -v linux32/steamclient.so /mnt/server/.steam/sdk32/steamclient.so
cp -v linux64/steamclient.so /mnt/server/.steam/sdk64/steamclient.so

if [[ ! -f "$HOME/ArmaReforgerServer" && ! -f "$HOME/ArmaReforgerServer.exe" ]]; then
    echo -e "\n\nSteamCMD failed to install Arma Reforger Dedicated Server!\n"
    exit 1
fi

mkdir -p $HOME/profile $HOME/tmp

## --- ArmaField additions ---

# Download static jq for the MapSeeding hook (one-shot, ~800 KB).
if [[ ! -f "$HOME/jq" ]]; then
    echo "Downloading jq for MapSeeding hook..."
    curl -sSL -o $HOME/jq https://github.com/jqlang/jq/releases/download/jq-1.7.1/jq-linux-amd64
    chmod +x $HOME/jq
fi

# Write the MapSeeding hook script.
cat > $HOME/apply_map_seeding.sh << 'HOOKEOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Read MapSeeding.json (written by the ArmaField mod between missions)
# and override game.scenarioId in config.json with the next mission.
# Falls back silently to whatever scenarioId is already in config.json.

SEED="./profile/profile/ArmaField/Systems/MapSeeding.json"

if [ ! -f "$SEED" ]; then
    echo "[ArmaField] No MapSeeding.json yet - using scenarioId from config.json"
    exit 0
fi

MISSION=$(./jq -r '.MissionResourceName // empty' "$SEED" 2>/dev/null)
MISSION="${MISSION#"${MISSION%%[![:space:]]*}"}"
MISSION="${MISSION%"${MISSION##*[![:space:]]}"}"

if [ -z "$MISSION" ]; then
    echo "[ArmaField] MapSeeding.json: MissionResourceName missing/empty - using scenarioId from config.json"
    exit 0
fi

case "$MISSION" in
    \{*\}*.conf) ;;
    *)
        echo "[ArmaField] MapSeeding.json: MissionResourceName=$MISSION doesn't look like a scenario resource - ignoring"
        exit 0
        ;;
esac

if ./jq --arg m "$MISSION" '.game.scenarioId = $m' config.json > config.json.tmp; then
    mv config.json.tmp config.json
    echo "[ArmaField] Loaded next mission from MapSeeding.json: $MISSION"
else
    rm -f config.json.tmp
    echo "[ArmaField] Failed to patch config.json - keeping existing scenarioId"
fi
HOOKEOF
chmod +x $HOME/apply_map_seeding.sh

# Default config.json (with ArmaField mod baked in) - created only if missing.
if [[ ! -f "$HOME/config.json" ]]; then
    echo "Creating default config.json with ArmaField mod..."
    cat > $HOME/config.json << EOF
{
    "bindAddress": "0.0.0.0",
    "bindPort": ${SERVER_PORT},
    "publicAddress": "${SERVER_IP}",
    "publicPort": ${SERVER_PORT},
    "a2s": {
        "address": "0.0.0.0",
        "port": 17777
    },
    "game": {
        "name": "${SERVER_NAME}",
        "password": "${SERVER_PASS}",
        "passwordAdmin": "${ADMIN_PASS}",
        "admins": [],
        "scenarioId": "${SCENARIO_ID}",
        "maxPlayers": ${MAX_PLAYERS},
        "visible": ${VISIBLE},
        "crossPlatform": false,
        "supportedPlatforms": ["PLATFORM_PC"],
        "gameProperties": {
            "serverMaxViewDistance": 2500,
            "serverMinGrassDistance": 50,
            "networkViewDistance": 1000,
            "disableThirdPerson": ${DISABLE_THIRD},
            "fastValidation": true,
            "battlEye": ${BATTLEYE},
            "VONDisableUI": true,
            "VONDisableDirectSpeechUI": true
        },
        "mods": [
            {
                "modId": "68FA258A6C74CE73",
                "name": "ArmaField"
            }
        ]
    },
    "operating": {
        "lobbyPlayerSynchronise": true,
        "joinQueue": {
            "maxSize": 50
        },
        "disableNavmeshStreaming": ["Soldiers"]
    }
}
EOF
fi

# BackendSettings.json - stub file so Pelican's JSON config parser can
# inject BACKEND_TOKEN / BACKEND_URL on every startup. Default values
# point at the public test backend; override via panel variables.
mkdir -p $HOME/profile/profile/ArmaField/Systems
if [[ ! -f "$HOME/profile/profile/ArmaField/Systems/BackendSettings.json" ]]; then
    cat > $HOME/profile/profile/ArmaField/Systems/BackendSettings.json << 'BEEOF'
{
    "ServerToken": "ARMAFIELD-TEST-TOKEN",
    "BackendURL": "https://test.armafield.gg"
}
BEEOF
fi

echo -e "\n\nArmaField Reforger server installed."
echo "Backend: defaults to public test backend (https://test.armafield.gg)."
echo "For production, set Backend Token and Backend URL in the panel variables.\n"

variables.name: Server Name
variables.description: Name displayed in the server browser.

variables.name: Server Password
variables.description: Password required for clients to enter the server. Leave empty for none.

variables.name: Admin Password
variables.description: Used with #login in chat for admin commands. No spaces.

variables.name: Max Players
variables.description: Maximum number of players allowed to join the server.

variables.name: Initial Scenario ID
variables.description: Starting mission used on first launch (or whenever MapSeeding.json is missing/invalid). The ArmaField mod will overwrite MapSeeding.json with the next mission after each round; that file takes precedence over this value on subsequent restarts.

variables.name: Visible in Server Browser
variables.description: Whether the server appears in the public server browser.

variables.name: Enable BattlEye
variables.description: Enables the anti-cheat engine. All clients must run BattlEye to connect.

variables.name: Disable Third Person
variables.description: Force first-person view for all players.

variables.name: Automatic Updates
variables.description: Quickly check for server/mod updates on startup and apply them.

variables.name: [Advanced] Max FPS
variables.description: Server FPS cap. Recommended 60-120. Leave empty for no cap.

variables.name: [Advanced] Log FPS Interval
variables.description: How often (in seconds) to log FPS/debug stats. 0 to disable.

variables.name: [Repair] Validate Server Files
variables.description: Set to "true" to validate all files when Automatic Updates runs. Significantly slows startup; only enable when fixing a broken install.

variables.name: ArmaField Backend URL
variables.description: HTTPS URL of the ArmaField backend the mod talks to. Default points at the public test backend (suitable for testing only). For production, point at your own self-hosted backend (https://github.com/ArmaField/ArmaField-BackEnd). Must start with https:// - Arma Reforger rejects plain http.

variables.name: ArmaField Backend Token
variables.description: Server token used to authenticate this server with the backend. Default is the public test token (paired with the test backend). For production, generate a strong random token, configure it on your self-hosted backend, and put the same value here.

variables.name: [Advanced] Steam App ID
variables.description: 1874900 = stable Arma Reforger Dedicated Server. 1890870 = experimental.
